data.description:
## Story S3: Same-session duplicate-launch guard with identity-verified liveness and flock

### Description
**What**: Before `start` launches a new child, check `<state_dir>/pid`. If present: (a) run `kill -0 <pid>` and (b) compare the live process cmdline against the snapshot at `<state_dir>/cmdline`. Both match → exit non-zero with an error whose body contains the exact override command string. Only `kill -0` succeeds but cmdline mismatches → auto-clear (PID was reused). Acquire `flock` on `<state_dir>/.launch-lock` before the liveness check and hold it through pid+cmdline write so rapid-fire invocations cannot both pass the check.
**Why**: Without identity-verified liveness, OS PID reuse can cause the launcher to refuse a legitimate new run (or kill an unrelated process via `--force`). Without flock, TOCTOU between check and write lets two rapid-fire `start` invocations both spawn children, orphaning the first.
**Scope**:
- IN: pid + cmdline-snapshot writes, identity-verified liveness check, `flock`-gated critical section, error message content
- OUT: stale-state auto-recovery when pid is missing (S5 — same story/file/module as this but a distinct DD), `--force` override (S6)

### Done Definitions
- When this story is complete, invoking `test-run start` while a liveness-verified active run exists exits non-zero with an error message containing the literal string `.claude/scripts/dso test-run start --force`
  ← Satisfies: "the launcher exits non-zero with an error message whose body contains the exact override command string (.claude/scripts/dso test-run start --force) so the agent can copy-paste it without guessing"
- When this story is complete, `kill -0 <pid>` succeeds but cmdline snapshot mismatch auto-clears the state dir (PID reuse case) rather than blocking the user
  ← Satisfies: "If kill -0 succeeds but cmdline does not match, auto-clear the state dir (stale-state recovery — PID was reused)"
- When this story is complete, two rapid-fire `test-run start` invocations in the same session cannot both pass the liveness check (flock serializes the critical section)
  ← Satisfies: "start acquires an flock on <state_dir>/.launch-lock before the liveness check and holds it through the pid write, preventing two rapid-fire test-run start invocations in the same session from both passing the check and spawning parallel orphans"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

### Considerations
- [Reliability] cmdline read source varies by OS (Linux `/proc/<pid>/cmdline`, macOS `ps -o command=`) — handle both platforms uniformly in the identity check
- [Testing] TOCTOU / race-condition tests should deliberately attempt concurrent launches and assert at most one succeeds
## Escalation Policy
**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
data.title: As a DSO agent, duplicate test-run launches in the same session are blocked via identity-verified liveness + flock
